user_prompt: add a `.gitignore` in `hardware` for a EAGLE 7.6.0 project
agent_reply: Added [hardware/.gitignore](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/hardware/.gitignore>) for EAGLE 7.6.0 transient files.
It ignores lock files, backup/temp files like `*.b#*`, `*.s#*`, `*.l#*`, and local EAGLE preference files, while leaving the actual project files visible:
```text
hardware/thefly.brd
hardware/thefly.lbr
hardware/thefly.sch

user_prompt: For the M5Stack Core2 as we have, what are all the I2C addresses being used?
agent_reply: Using 7-bit I2C addresses, the Core2 internal bus is SDA `21` / SCL `22` per [inc/pins.h](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/pins.h:16>).
Expected/active Core2 addresses:
| Address | Device | Notes |

| `0x34` | AXP192 PMIC | Used by M5Unified and directly by our speaker GPIO2 fallback in [AudioManager.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1018>). Core2 v1.1 AXP2101 is also `0x34`. |
| `0x38` | FT5x06 / FT6336 touch | M5GFX Core2 touch config uses this. |
| `0x51` | BM8563 / PCF8563 RTC | M5Unified RTC default. |

| `0x68` | MPU6886 IMU | Conditional: our code notes the IMU is on a daughterboard and may disappear with another codec board; it probes internal then external I2C in [ImuOrientation.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ImuOrientation/ImuOrientation.cpp:1>). |
Also on our schematic, but not currently implemented in firmware:
| Address | Device | Notes |

| `0x0A` | SGTL5000XNLA3 external audio codec | Schematic connects `CTRL_DATA`/`CTRL_CLK` to the I2C control bus in [hardware/thefly.sch](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/hardware/thefly.sch:2571>). The address comes from the NXP SGTL5000 datasheet: the 20-QFN part uses `0001010(R/W)`, i.e. 7-bit `0x0A`. Our `AudioManager` still says external I2S codec setup is not implemented. |

So if you run an I2C scanner on this exact setup, I’d expect `0x34`, `0x38`, `0x51`, `0x68`, and possibly `0x0A` if the SGTL5000 board is installed and powered. Source for SGTL5000 address: [NXP SGTL5000 datasheet](https://cache.nxp.com/docs/en/data-sheet/SGTL5000.pdf).

The address comes from the NXP SGTL5000 datasheet: the 20-QFN part uses `0001010(R/W)`, i.e. 7-bit `0x0A`. Our `AudioManager` still says external I2S codec setup is not implemented. |

commit_message: pcb layout changes, removed L4, added comments
pcb layout use ledc pwm mclk
layout change to add dc block on mic path
pcb layout removed agnd, more caps

user_prompt: so it initialized fine, and the earbud_adc is still stuck at 4095
these boards are easy to swap and I swapped in a working one, and it, well, works.
are you able to look at the schematic?
just to sanity check your ability, what is the purpose of R1, R2, and R9?
agent_reply: Yes. I looked at [hardware/thefly.sch](/D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/hardware/thefly.sch:2299) and the local ExtCodec notes.
- `R1` is `2K2`: mic-bias feed from SGTL5000 `MIC_BIAS` to `HP-MIC`. It biases an inline/electret mic; the mic signal is then AC-coupled through `C7` into the codec mic input.
- `R2` is `1M`: weak pull-up from `VDDA` to the `MIC_BIAS` node. It helps define/bias that inline-mic detect/mic-bias network.

- `R9` is `1M`: pull-up from `HP-SENSE-L` to `3.3V`. `HP-SENSE-L` goes to the jack detect contact and to the earbud ADC path, which firmware reads on GPIO35 in [pins.h](/D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/pins.h:24).

So for your stuck `4095`: that is very plausibly not codec init. `R9` is literally supposed to pull the earbud sense line high; the jack/sense contact should change that when unplugged. I’d suspect the jack sense switch/contact, solder on `J1` pin 5 or related contact, continuity from `HP-SENSE-L`, or an accidental short/leak to `3.3V`.
